verses.1: Then the word of the LORD came to me, saying,
verses.2: “Now you, son of man, take up a lament for Tyre.
verses.3: Tell Tyre, who dwells at the gateway to the sea, merchant of the peoples on many coasts, that this is what the Lord GOD says:
verses.12: Tarshish was your merchant because of your great wealth of goods; they exchanged silver, iron, tin, and lead for your wares.
verses.13: Javan, Tubal, and Meshech were your merchants. They exchanged slaves and bronze utensils for your merchandise.
verses.14: The men of Beth-togarmah exchanged horses, war horses, and mules for your wares.
verses.15: The men of Dedan were your clients; many coastlands were your market; they paid you with ivory tusks and ebony.
verses.16: Aram was your customer because of your many products; they exchanged turquoise, purple, embroidered work, fine linen, coral, and rubies for your wares.
verses.17: Judah and the land of Israel traded with you; they exchanged wheat from Minnith, cakes and honey, oil and balm for your merchandise.
verses.18: Because of your many products and your great wealth of goods, Damascus traded with you wine from Helbon, wool from Zahar,
verses.19: and casks of wine from Izal for your wares. Wrought iron, cassia, and sweet cane were exchanged for your merchandise.
verses.20: Dedan was your merchant in saddlecloths for riding.
verses.21: Arabia and all the princes of Kedar were your customers, trading in lambs, rams, and goats.
verses.22: The merchants of Sheba and Raamah traded with you; for your wares they exchanged gold, the finest of all spices, and precious stones.
verses.23: Haran, Canneh, and Eden traded with you, and so did the merchants of Sheba, Asshur, and Chilmad.
verses.24: In your marketplace they traded with you fine garments of blue, embroidered work, and multicolored rugs with cords tightly twisted and knotted.
